Division of Globe XIV 169
Samata^a is associated with Davaka (Nogong district of Assam)
and Kamarupa (Assam) in Samudragupta's Allahabad Pra^asti. In
the time of the Khadga rulers Karmmanta (modern Kamta near
Comilla) was the capital of Samatata.
Pragjyotifa is the region around Gauhati. . According to the
Raghuvarhsa (IV. 81) it lay on the other bank of the Lauhitya.
Hemacandra tells us in his Abhidhnnacintzmani (IV. 22) that this
place is the same as Kamarupa.
Paundra corresponds to North Bengal with its capital at
Mahasthan in the Bogra district.
Gaudaka comprised the present Murshidabad district. Some-
times it means the whole of Bengal. Others hold that Western
Bengal came to be called Gauda from the city of the same name.
